headline: Vernacular School Funding Cut 18% in Budget 2026
context: Chinese and Tamil vernacular schools saw allocation reductions in the 2026 federal budget despite enrollment increases.

cards.big: 1,302 vernacular schools serve 21% of Malaysia's primary students
cards.sub: Budget cuts affect infrastructure repairs and teacher placements

cards.big: SJK(C) schools received RM80 million less than 2025 allocation
cards.sub: Capital development grants dropped from RM450M to RM370M

cards.big: Article 152 protects the right to teach in mother tongue languages
cards.sub: Constitutional guarantee coexists with national education policy tensions

cards.big: 37% of SJK(C) school buildings are over 50 years old
cards.sub: Sarawak and Perak report worst infrastructure conditions

cards.big: Funding equity for all school types strengthens national unity, not weakens it
Does equal per-student funding across all school streams make sense
